title: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent
display_title: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent
wikitext:
[]: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Mon May 20, 2024 9:42 pm AFAIK, none of the English dubs of Evangelion ever adapted Touji's Kansai accent, but what if they did? The choices New Yorker/Brooklynite (the Viz release went in this route) Southern Redneck British Chav Australian Bogan Northern British like Scouse (i.e. Liverpool) and Geordie (aka Newcastle or Tyneside) Cockney (aka Southeast London)

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu May 23, 2024 11:31 pm I think I've heard somewhere that he had a New Yorker accent in the English dub (at least in one of them I guess). Isn't that the case? Or is it just a manga thing? I actually never watched it in English...

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Fri May 24, 2024 11:55 pm BernardoCairo wrote: I think I've heard somewhere that he had a New Yorker accent in the English dub (at least in one of them I guess). Isn't that the case? Or is it just a manga thing?

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon May 27, 2024 9:13 am Oh, I didn't know that. Thanks for sharing it with me. I think a New Yorker accent is actually perfect for him. It has nothing to do with Osaka, sure. But Toji's character really gives off that street vibe. Both because of the way he acts and his tracksuit...

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Tue May 28, 2024 1:37 am BernardoCairo wrote: Oh, I didn't know that. Thanks for sharing it with me. I think a New Yorker accent is actually perfect for him. It has nothing to do with Osaka, sure. But Toji's character really gives off that street vibe. Both because of the way he acts and his tracksuit...

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed May 29, 2024 1:29 pm C. Smith wrote: OTOH, in Japanese society, a tracksuit is kind of used as a shorthand for someone who's from the sticks, usually in reference to the rather tacky styles of Japanese living in rural or less developed areas.

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Fri May 31, 2024 11:41 pm BernardoCairo wrote: Well, that makes sense considering it's the suit Shinji wore while in Village-3 in Shin (it's a very rural and poor place, of course). Toji never struck me as a particular rich guy, especially if you consider what he had for lunch... But then again, the basketball thing and his cap also really help sell that street vibe. I think he might be poor, but not necessarily a rural guy. That's why I wouldn't give him a Southern Redneck accent. Maybe a ghetto accent would go well?

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jun 12, 2024 10:45 pm I think the punk accent could actually work, but you would have to change his design to go along with it. Maybe make it a bit more "Akira"? I wouldn't really like that though. It would be funny to see Toji with a British accent by the way. I don't know why but I think he would find the British accent to be silly

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by VeryConfusedDingus » Thu Jun 13, 2024 2:44 pm If we're going with "The Idiot from Osaka" trope as a basis then I think a British chav accent could translate well. You still get chavs in rural areas. A rough working-class Bristolian, Swindon or broadly Southwest Country accent maybe? A mix between country and street. It would suit Touji's character. My concern with that would be it sounding weird in contrast considering most characters in the English dub will likely have American accents. So maybe a slight Southern accent would work better?

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Tue Jun 18, 2024 7:46 pm VeryConfusedDingus wrote: If we're going with "The Idiot from Osaka" trope as a basis then I think a British chav accent could translate well. You still get chavs in rural areas. A rough working-class Bristolian, Swindon or broadly Southwest Country accent maybe? A mix between country and street.

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Fri Jun 21, 2024 12:08 am The Miami accent is perfect! I can totally see him playing basketball on the beaches of Florida I think he is weird enough to be a Florida man lol!

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by C. Smith » Sat Jun 22, 2024 5:14 pm BernardoCairo wrote: The Miami accent is perfect! I can totally see him playing basketball on the beaches of Florida I think he is weird enough to be a Florida man lol!

[]: Re: What accent should Touji Suzuhara speak if he ever had one in the English for his Osaka accent?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Jun 24, 2024 5:02 pm I agree! What he did when Asuka forat showed up ... I mean, that was very Florida Man like. Although I do wonder, is he weird enough? Well, he and Kensuke did almost die trying to see a battle by themselves for no good reason. So he is stupid! But he can get very serious when it's needed and has a huge heart. So there's more to his character I guess.
